mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and raises equity multiples, so a downward monthly trend in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS has declined for at least 2 consecutive months, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated FEDFUNDS level above neutral tightens financial conditions and suppresses equity valuation expansion, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days after the policy rate crosses a high-rate threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate above neutral threshold constrains risk assets
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker future demand, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor signal does not outperform either single proxy alone in 5-day forward return prediction, or if the interaction term is not statistically distinct from zero in rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in RSAFS above recent trend signals resilient consumer spending and revenue support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds its prior 3-month average by 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales acceleration does not coincide with positive abnormal 20-day SPX returns relative to unconditional baseline, or if the signal flips sign in more than one third of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO together with firmer HOUST signals broad real activity and improving cyclically sensitive earnings prospects, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint industrial-production and housing expansion does not produce positive 60-day forward returns, or if the interaction effect is weaker than the best single-proxy model across rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent baseline reflects a housing-demand slowdown that foreshadows broader consumption weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start contractions fail to precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered periods produce positive returns more often than negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is still elevated while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates are negative and financial conditions are easy; that combination predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-policy interaction does not improve 5-day forecasting versus either series alone, or if positive returns are not more frequent when CPIAUCSL is high and FEDFUNDS is low.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE level signals tight labor markets and stable household income, which supports near-term equity demand and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment below 5.0% does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal fails to beat a no-signal benchmark in walk-forward testing.
